title: Assemblymember Bauer-Kahan advances AB 2626 out of the Assembly Committee on Revenue and Taxation with bi-partisan support to bring financial relief to school districts purchasing

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The COVID-19 pandemic has fundamentally changed the landscape of education.",
    "One in five California students, which is approximately 1.2 million kids, lack broadband or a computer to access distance learning.",
    "AB 2626 helps close the digital divide by exempting all school districts from state sales tax when purchasing equipment associated with distance learning.",
    "AB 2626 aims to level the playing field and bring equitable access to distance learning for all California students.",
    "Reducing the upfront cost of devices and technology can help school districts transition to distance learning."
  ]
}
```
